title: halimium halimifolium subsp. rhiphaeum
intro: Halimium halimifolium subsp. rhiphaeum, commonly known as Rock Rose or Cistus, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Cistaceae. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional folk medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Halimium halimifolium subsp. rhiphaeum include its potential to soothe wounds, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas and infusions, are used to promote wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
